extraction.one_line_summary: MCNULTY Brandon soloed to victory from a 10-rider breakaway after attacking the final classified climb 45km out, gaining nearly 2 minutes while Roglic inherited yellow as Schachmann cracked.
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma controlled for Roglic despite massive non-starter attrition weakening all teams, revealing depth advantage in depleted races.
McNulty's attack on final classified climb with 45km remaining was decisive before technical descent, not the unclassified ramp at 12km.
Ineos attempted isolation attacks on Roglic after teammates dropped but Martinez couldn't get room, suggesting Jumbo neutralization even when isolated.
Schachmann's crack on classified climb handed yellow to Roglic without him attacking, revealing Paris-Nice GC often won passively through attrition.

rider_signals.MCNULTY Brandon: positive: At 23, bridged from breakaway domestique role to WorldTour stage winner by recognizing when to counter-attack on classified climb.
rider_signals.SCHACHMANN Max: negative: Lost yellow jersey and dropped from GC podium by cracking on relatively modest classified climb with 45km remaining.
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: Inherited yellow through defensive riding after all teammates dropped, never needed to counter Yates/Vlasov/Martinez attacks.
rider_signals.MARTINEZ Daniel: negative: Ineos gave him opportunity to attack isolated Roglic but couldn't force meaningful gaps despite numerical advantage.
rider_signals.JORGENSEN Matteo: positive: Gained significant GC time by making chase group behind McNulty, showing Movistar developing stage-race depth beyond leaders.
